authorities.name: Building & Construction Authority (BCA)
authorities.role: Statutory regulator of Singapore's built environment — sets Building Control Act, supervision regime, builder/firm licensing, sustainability and quality benchmarks.
authorities.headcount_or_remit: Lead regulator for site supervision firm licensing from 2028

authorities.name: Ministry of Manpower (MOM)
authorities.role: Owns workforce policy — Employment Act, Work Permit/S Pass/EP, foreign worker quotas, levies, salary thresholds, WSH Act.

authorities.name: Singapore Accreditation Council (SAC)
authorities.role: National accreditation body under Enterprise Singapore — accredits inspection bodies (ISO/IEC 17020), certification bodies, calibration & testing labs.
authorities.headcount_or_remit: Accreditation gateway for BCA firm-based licensing

authorities.name: IES/ACES Joint Accreditation Committee (JAC)
authorities.role: Industry-tripartite committee accrediting individual Resident Engineers (RE) and Resident Technical Officers (RTO) for site supervision.

authorities.name: SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG)
authorities.role: Statutory board governing the WSQ framework, ATO accreditation, SkillsFuture Credit, and SCTP/CCP reskilling.

authorities.name: Workforce Singapore (WSG)
authorities.role: Statutory board for workforce development — runs Career Conversion Programmes, IHRP, and employer-side workforce schemes.

authorities.name: Professional Engineers Board (PEB)
authorities.role: Registers Professional Engineers in civil, structural, electrical, mechanical, chemical engineering — issues practising certificates required for QP(D) and QP(S) roles.
authorities.headcount_or_remit: Issues annual practising certificates with 40 PDU CPD requirement

authorities.name: Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA)
authorities.role: National cybersecurity authority — administers Cyber Essentials and Cyber Trust marks for enterprise certification.

authorities.name: Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC)
authorities.role: Administers PDPA 2012 — including the data breach notification regime active since 1 Feb 2021.

authorities.name: Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA)
authorities.role: Issues Data Protection Trustmark (DPTM) certification (now SS 714:2025) jointly with PDPC.

authorities.name: Workplace Safety & Health Council (WSHC)
authorities.role: Tripartite council under MOM — administers bizSAFE 5-level certification, sets WSH best-practice standards.

authorities.name: Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore (IRAS)
authorities.role: Administers Skills Development Levy and Progressive Wage Credit Scheme disbursements alongside CPF Board.

authorities.name: Central Provident Fund Board (CPFB)
authorities.role: Administers CPF Act — employer & employee contributions, senior worker rate changes, SDL collection.

authorities.name: Singapore Green Building Council (SGBC)
authorities.role: Administers Green Mark Professional (GMA / GMAP / GMAAP) qualifications for sustainability roles in built environment.

authorities.name: Institute for Human Resource Professionals (IHRP)
authorities.role: Tripartite-set HR professional body (MOM/NTUC/SNEF) — issues IHRP-CA / IHRP-CP / IHRP-SP / IHRP-MP credentials.

regulations.title: Firm-Based Licensing Regime for Site Supervision of Structural Works
regulations.applies_to: Site supervision services on building projects valued above S$75 million
regulations.summary: BCA announced on 3 Sep 2025 a firm-based licensing regime for site supervision firms, mandatory from 2028 for projects above S$75M. Firms must first secure SAC accreditation, then a BCA licence, demonstrating technical competence, governance systems and structured staff development.
details: Pillar 1 — Firm-level governance: ISO/IEC 17020 quality system, document management, retention of supervision records, structured training & career pathways.
Pillar 2 — Key personnel: Technical Director (registered PE), QP(S) appointments, permanent core team of REs & RTOs (JAC-accredited) with sustained employment.
Pillar 3 — Operational/SOP/tech: calibrated measurement equipment, digital recordkeeping, video-streaming/remote-supervision capabilities, methodologies aligned with the SSP.
Individual professionals may continue serving projects outside the regime's scope (i.e. <S$75M) without firm licence.
BCA encourages voluntary engagement of accredited firms ahead of the 2028 mandatory date.
regulations.elitez_implication: This is the core regime Elitez Site Supervisor is being built around. Q1–Q2 2028 firm-based licence is the target; SAC accreditation pathway opens 2026 for early movers.

official_urls.label: BCA Awards Dinner Media Release PDF
BCA Public Consultation Paper — firm-based proposal

regulations.title: Firm-Based Licensing Regime for Lift & Escalator Certification
regulations.applies_to: Modern lift/escalator systems designed to SS550:2009 and SS626:2017 or later (excludes home lifts and older systems)
regulations.summary: Sister regime to the structural-works firm licence — mandatory from 2027 for L&E certification firms. Sets the procedural template (SAC accreditation gate, then BCA licence) that the 2028 site-supervision regime follows.
details: SAC accreditation scheme for L&E certification firms launched April 2025.
Voluntary engagement of accredited firms encouraged ahead of the 2027 mandatory date.
Individual specialists may continue certifying older lift/escalator systems outside scope.
regulations.elitez_implication: Not directly applicable to Elitez Site Supervisor's structural-supervision scope, but the L&E rollout is the live precedent — SAC and BCA processes hardened on it before 2028.

official_urls.label: BCA — Firm-Based Regime for Lift and Escalator Certification

regulations.title: Building Control Act 1989
regulations.applies_to: All building works in Singapore
regulations.summary: Primary legislation governing building works. Section 8 (design) and Section 11 (supervision) impose duties on Qualified Persons; supervisors must take all reasonable steps to ensure works comply with approved plans, the Act and regulations, and must report contraventions to the Commissioner of Building Control.
details: Section 11 — duties of builders and the QP(S) supervision regime.
Building Control (Accreditation of Site Supervisors) Regulations operationalise mandatory JAC accreditation since 15 Feb 2009.
Empowers BCA to make subsidiary regulations (Buildability, Builders Licensing, etc.).
regulations.elitez_implication: Core statutory backbone — every Elitez deployment of supervisors is a Section 11 appointment under the BCA's eye.

regulations.title: Site Supervision Plan (SSP) — Mandatory Submission
regulations.applies_to: Building projects with works value above S$7.5 million (mandatory SSP submission); below threshold a copy must still be kept on site.
regulations.summary: BCA mandated submission of a formal Site Supervision Plan with the permit application for structural and geotechnical works above S$7.5M. The SSP details supervision methodologies, frequency, and checklists.
details: SSP jointly published guidebook by BCA, IES & ACES.
Below S$7.5M — SSP not submitted but the appointed QP must still keep a copy on site.
SSP forms the operational handshake between the QP(S), the supervision firm and BCA inspectors.
regulations.elitez_implication: Every regulated deployment relies on a credible SSP — the supervision firm's SOPs must mirror the SSP's methodology to pass BCA inspection.

regulations.title: Builders Licensing Scheme (BLS)
regulations.applies_to: Main contractors carrying out general/specialist building works (Class 1 = unlimited; Class 2 ≤ S$6M)
regulations.summary: Separate from the new firm-based supervision licence — the BLS regulates builders/contractors. Requires Approved Person (AP) and Technical Controller (TC). Class 1 mandates a corporation entity. Distinct from but adjacent to the Site Supervision firm licence.
details: Class 1 General Builder — projects of any value.
Class 2 General Builder — projects up to S$6M, sole proprietorship/partnership eligible.
Application via eBACS portal, processed within 14 working days.
Specialist builder enhancements proposed in 2024 REACH consultation.
regulations.elitez_implication: Elitez Site Supervisor will not be a builder, but its clients (main contractors) must be BLS-licensed. Reference scheme for understanding the BCA firm-licensing toolkit.

regulations.title: Public Sector Panels of Consultants (PSPC)
regulations.applies_to: Consultancy firms tendering for public sector construction projects
regulations.summary: Listing of consultancy firms eligible to tender for public sector construction services. Each discipline split into Panels 1–4 with different tendering limits. Procurement uses Quality-Fee Method (QFM); consortium bidding allowed across panels.
details: Panel structure 1–4 sets project-value tendering limit per discipline.
Quality Fee Method (QFM) — competitive selection on quality + fee proposal.
Smaller-panel firms can pool to bid on higher-panel tenders.
PSPC Terms of Listing last revised 1 Jan 2024.
regulations.elitez_implication: The PSPC pathway frames how government-procured supervision retainers are won; Elitez positioning toward Grade upgrades is part of the long-game.

regulations.title: Price Quality Method (PQM) — incl. mandatory Environmental Sustainability criterion
regulations.applies_to: Public sector construction services tenders with estimated cost above S$50 million, called on or after 31 Jan 2025
regulations.summary: PQM is BCA's standard tender evaluation framework. Effective 31 Jan 2025, an environmental sustainability criterion (climate disclosure + decarbonisation of construction processes) is mandatory in the Quality component for tenders above S$50M.
details: Sustainability sub-criteria: climate-related disclosures aligned with international standards; decarbonisation plans (e.g. electrification of construction equipment).
PQM framework last revised 1 Nov 2024.
Distinct from the Buildability score (design-stage compliance).
regulations.elitez_implication: Site supervision firms tendering on >S$50M public projects must show climate disclosure & decarb capability — Elitez should bake ESG metrics into proposal templates.

official_urls.label: BCA Circular on Environmental Sustainability Criteria in PQM

regulations.title: Buildable Design (B-Score) & Constructability (C-Score) Framework
regulations.applies_to: Building projects with Gross Floor Area (GFA) ≥ 5,000 m²
regulations.summary: Building Control (Buildability and Productivity) Regulations 2011 require minimum B-Score (productive design technologies) and C-Score (labour-saving construction methods) for projects with GFA ≥ 5,000 m².
details: B-Score evaluated at design stage; C-Score at construction stage.
Code of Practice on Buildability sets project-type-specific minimums.
Distinct from PQM — Buildability is a design-stage compliance gate, PQM is tender-stage scoring.
regulations.elitez_implication: Site supervisors verify on the ground that approved buildable methods (precast, system formwork) are actually deployed — a competency that should be in REs' job description.

regulations.title: BCA Green Mark Certification (GM:2021)
regulations.applies_to: All new buildings; new public sector buildings with >5,000 m² air-conditioned floor area must attain Platinum.
regulations.summary: Green Mark certifies sustainability across Energy Efficiency, Whole Life Carbon, Health & Wellbeing, Resilience, Intelligence and Maintainability. GM:2021 raised the bar — Platinum needs ≥55% energy improvement vs 2005 baseline. Ratings: Certified, Gold, GoldPLUS, Platinum.
details: GM:2021 introduces six pillars; aligned with UN SDGs.
Platinum mandatory for new public-sector buildings >5,000 m² air-conditioned area.
Feeds the PQM environmental sustainability criterion.
regulations.elitez_implication: Site supervision firms verifying Green Mark deliverables on site need GMA/GMAP-credentialled staff — links to SGBC accreditation.

regulations.title: CONQUAS — Construction Quality Assessment System
regulations.applies_to: Public sector projects (mandatory); private sector adopts widely as quality benchmark.
regulations.summary: CONQUAS is the de-facto national workmanship quality benchmark, established 1989. Companion Quality Mark (QM) Scheme since 2002 assesses every unit of new private residential projects (≥851/1000 = QM certified).
details: CONQUAS for Public Projects — mandatory in public-sector tenders.
Quality Mark — unit-by-unit assessment of new residential.
Certified Quality Mark / CONQUAS Supervisor & Manager Schemes (with SCAL) for QC personnel.
regulations.elitez_implication: Quality KPIs from CONQUAS feed back into supervision firms' performance scorecards — Elitez should track CONQUAS outcomes per supervised project.

regulations.title: Construction Workforce Registration System (CWRS) / CoreTrade & Multi-Skilling
regulations.applies_to: Construction trade workers — for upgrading to Higher-Skilled / Direct R1 status
regulations.summary: Online registry for CoreTrade and Multi-Skilling registrations and renewals. CoreTrade certifies workers as competent in a specific trade (skill-tested via Skills Evaluation Certificate). Drives the workforce mix BCA demands of contractors and supervisors.
details: Stage 1: trade & class application via CWRS.
Stage 2: SEC(K) skills assessment via BCA-approved ATTC or BCA Academy.
Stage 3: status/expiry tracked in CWRS.
Processed within 7 working days.
regulations.elitez_implication: Elitez does not register workers itself, but supervisors must spot-check that contractors deploy CoreTrade-certified labour — an audit function.

regulations.title: Construction Sector Dependency Ratio Ceiling (DRC) — 1:5
regulations.applies_to: All construction-sector employers hiring Work Permit Holders
regulations.summary: Construction DRC reduced from 1:7 (87.5%) to 1:5 (83.3%) effective 1 Jan 2024 — for every 5 Work Permit holders, the firm must employ 1 local earning ≥ LQS. Man-Year Entitlement framework removed concurrently.
details: DRC reduced from 87.5% (1:7) to 83.3% (1:5) on 1 Jan 2024.
MYE (Man-Year Entitlement) abolished — main contractors no longer apply for MYE based on contract value.
Drives mechanisation / automation adoption.
regulations.elitez_implication: Elitez's value prop — supplying credentialled local REs/RTOs — directly helps clients lift their local headcount and unlock more WP slots.

official_urls.label: BCA-MOM Circular on Construction WPH policies
MOM — Construction Sector Work Permit rules

regulations.title: S Pass Minimum Qualifying Salary
regulations.applies_to: New S Pass applications and renewals
regulations.summary: S Pass minimum salary rises from S$3,300 to S$3,600 (S$3,800 → S$4,000 financial services) from 1 Jan 2027 for new applications, with renewals phased in 2028. Announced in Budget 2026 by PM Wong (17 Feb 2026).
details: Current (2026): S$3,300 baseline / S$3,800 financial services.
From 1 Jan 2027: S$3,600 baseline / S$4,000 financial services.
From 1 Jan 2028: same thresholds extended to renewals of older holders.
regulations.elitez_implication: Squeezes the S Pass band common for foreign supervisors — pushes Elitez toward local-RE hiring or higher-paid mid-career foreigners.

regulations.title: Employment Pass Minimum Qualifying Salary
regulations.applies_to: New EP applications and renewals
regulations.summary: EP minimum salary rises from S$5,600 to S$6,000 (S$6,200 → S$6,600 financial services) from 1 Jan 2027 for new applications, renewals from 2028.
details: Current (2026): S$5,600 / S$6,200 financial services.
From 1 Jan 2027: S$6,000 / S$6,600 financial services.
Renewals phased into 2028.
COMPASS framework still applies separately.
regulations.elitez_implication: Affects QP(S) and Technical Director recruitment — foreign-sourced PEs must clear S$6,000 from 2027.

regulations.title: Local Qualifying Salary (LQS) — S$1,800
regulations.applies_to: All employers hiring Work Permit / S Pass holders (DRC headcount calc)
regulations.summary: LQS — the floor for a local employee to count toward DRC quota — rises from S$1,600 to S$1,800/month from 1 Jul 2026 (Budget 2026). Locals earning S$900–S$1,800 count as half-headcount.
details: Full local headcount: ≥ S$1,800/month.
Half local headcount: S$900 ≤ wage < S$1,800.
PWCS co-funding raised to 30% for 2026 to ease transition.
regulations.elitez_implication: Elitez must price local-supervisor packages at ≥ S$1,800 to count toward client DRC — sets the floor for entry-level local talent supply.

regulations.title: S Pass Levy — Harmonised Flat S$650
regulations.applies_to: All S Pass employers (cross-sector)
regulations.summary: Since 1 Sep 2025 the S Pass levy is harmonised at a flat S$650/month across all sectors (Tier 1 rose from S$550 to S$650; Tier 2 stayed S$650). Simplifies cross-sector cost planning.
details: Pre-Sep 2025: Tier 1 S$550 / Tier 2 S$650 (Services).
From 1 Sep 2025: flat S$650 across all sectors and tiers.
Aligns S Pass cost with local Associate Professionals & Technicians wage benchmarks.
regulations.elitez_implication: Predictable S Pass cost line item; combined with the 2027 salary floor, pushes the all-in S Pass cost up materially.

official_urls.label: MOM — Levy rates for S Pass

regulations.title: Work Permit Levy Framework Simplification (2028 onward)
regulations.applies_to: Manufacturing and Services sector Work Permit employers
regulations.summary: MOM will simplify the Work Permit levy framework — currently 24 rates/tiers — first to 20, with progressive consolidation. Manufacturing & Services tier restructuring takes effect from 2028.
details: Step 1: 24 rates → 20 rates.
Step 2: further streamlining to fewer tiers post-2028.
Lower levies steered toward higher-skilled/productive workers.
regulations.elitez_implication: Indirect — affects construction-sector clients' overall workforce cost stack, not Elitez's licensed-supervisor headcount directly.

regulations.title: Skills Development Levy (SDL)
regulations.applies_to: All Singapore employers (including foreign workers, capped per head)
regulations.summary: 0.25% of monthly total wages, minimum S$2 (≤S$800 wage), maximum S$11.25 (≥S$4,500 wage). Channelled into Skills Development Fund supporting WSQ and CET training subsidies.
details: Rate set since October 2008.
Late payment penalty — 10% per annum on outstanding amount.
Funds SkillsFuture training subsidies.
regulations.elitez_implication: Standard payroll line item; funds the very training subsidies (SCTP/CCP) that Elitez relies on for reskilling pipelines.

regulations.title: Central Provident Fund Act — incl. Senior Worker Increase Jan 2027
regulations.applies_to: All CPF-eligible employees (Singapore citizens & PRs)
regulations.summary: Standard employer rate is 17% (capped at OW S$7,400/month). Senior worker rates step up — from 1 Jan 2027 employer rate +0.5pp for ages 55–65; total rate +1.5pp for ages 55–60 and +1pp for 60–65. Government offsets half of the 2027 employer increase.
details: Standard employer rate: 17% (≤55).
From 1 Jan 2027: ages >55–60 total rate +1.5pp; ages >60–65 total rate +1pp.
Increase fully allocated to Retirement Account up to FRS.
Senior Employment Credit (SEC) and CPF Transition Offset (CTO) cushion employer cost.
regulations.elitez_implication: Locks in higher payroll cost for older REs/RTOs (a common profile); offset partially funded.

official_urls.label: CPFB — CPF Contribution Changes from 1 Jan 2027

regulations.title: Workplace Safety & Health Act 2006 — incl. Construction Regulations 2007
regulations.applies_to: All workplaces; Construction Regulations apply to construction worksites specifically
regulations.summary: Primary occupational safety legislation. Imposes duty of care on every person at the workplace. Workplace Safety & Health (Construction) Regulations 2007 are the prescriptive sub-legislation for construction sites.
details: Duty-holder framework — occupiers, employers, principals, contractors, employees.
Risk Management Regulations — mandatory hazard ID/RA/RC.
Incident reporting via WSH Council.
regulations.elitez_implication: REs/RTOs are part of the duty-holder chain — their training and SOPs must reference WSH Construction Regs explicitly.

regulations.title: Work Injury Compensation Act 2019 (WICA)
regulations.applies_to: Employers of all manual workers (any salary) and non-manual workers earning ≤ S$2,600/month
regulations.summary: Mandates work injury compensation insurance for all manual workers and non-manual workers ≤ S$2,600/month. Provides a no-fault, low-cost alternative to common-law tort claims for work-related injuries.
details: Insurance must be issued by a designated insurer with MOM-compulsory terms.
Failure to insure: fine and/or imprisonment.
Construction supervisors qualify as manual workers — insurance mandatory regardless of wage.
regulations.elitez_implication: Non-negotiable; baseline insurance line item for every supervisor on payroll.

regulations.title: bizSAFE Certification (5-Level Programme)
regulations.applies_to: Companies tendering in construction, manufacturing, marine, logistics — voluntary in law, contractually mandatory in practice.
regulations.summary: 5-level programme administered by WSH Council building WSH culture: Level 1 (top-mgmt commitment) → Level 2 (RM Champion) → Level 3 (RM implementation) → Level Star (SS 506 / OHSAS/ISO 45001) → Level Partner. De-facto required for government tenders and most construction sub-contracts.
details: Level 3 audit by MOM-approved auditor.
Star = ISO 45001 / SS 506 certified (annual surveillance).
Pathway is referenced in many tender pre-qualifications.
regulations.elitez_implication: Elitez itself should target bizSAFE Level 3+ to qualify for prime-contractor sub-contracts and government work.

regulations.title: Progressive Wage Credit Scheme (PWCS)
regulations.applies_to: Singapore citizen employees with gross monthly wage ≤ S$3,000 (2025/26 ceiling)
regulations.summary: Government co-funds wage increases for lower-wage Singaporeans. Budget 2026 raised 2026 co-funding from 20% to 30% and extended PWCS through 2028 (30% in 2027, 20% in 2028). Wage ceiling raised from S$2,500 to S$3,000.
details: Auto-disbursed via IRAS — no application.
2025–2026 wage ceiling: S$3,000.
2027–2028 minimum qualifying increase: S$200 (was S$100).
Stacks with PWM/LQS schemes.
regulations.elitez_implication: Subsidy on wage uplifts for entry-level local supervisors — directly improves Elitez's local-talent value-prop unit economics.

regulations.title: SAC Accreditation Scheme for Site Supervision Firms (ISO/IEC 17020)
regulations.applies_to: Firms seeking BCA Site Supervision Firm Licence
regulations.summary: SAC will launch the inspection-body accreditation scheme for site supervision firms in 2026, pre-requisite to the 2028 BCA firm licence. Built on ISO/IEC 17020 (general inspection-body standard).
details: Mirrors the L&E firm scheme launched April 2025.
Assesses technical competence, impartiality, governance, document control.
ISO/IEC 17020:2026 transition arrangements apply (per UKAS technical bulletin).
regulations.elitez_implication: This is the gating credential — Elitez must scope ISO/IEC 17020 management system implementation by mid-2026 to enter the voluntary track and be among the first licensed in 2028.

regulations.title: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management
regulations.applies_to: Any organisation seeking certified QMS
regulations.summary: Voluntary international QMS standard widely required in PSPC quality scoring and PQM tender evaluation. Accredited via SAC-MS scheme.
details: ISO 9001:2015 — risk-based thinking, leadership clauses.
PQM and PSPC tender packs reference QMS in the Quality component.
Foundational layer for ISO/IEC 17020.
regulations.elitez_implication: Implementing ISO 9001 first is the conventional stepping-stone toward the more demanding ISO/IEC 17020 inspection-body system.

regulations.title: ISO/IEC 27001 Information Security Management
regulations.applies_to: Any organisation handling sensitive data; commonly required by larger clients & government tenders
regulations.summary: International ISMS standard — accredited certification via SAC-MS. Increasingly cited in BCA digital-storage and remote-streaming requirements under the new firm regime.
details: Annex A controls cover access control, supplier security, incident response.
Surveillance audits annually; recertification every 3 years.
Aligns with CSA Cyber Trust mark domains.
regulations.elitez_implication: Site supervision firm regime requires digital recordkeeping and video streaming — ISO 27001 is the natural data-handling control framework.

regulations.title: ISO 14001 Environmental Management
regulations.applies_to: Organisations with environmental management obligations or tendering on PQM environmental criteria
regulations.summary: International EMS standard. Strengthens credibility under PQM environmental-sustainability criterion (mandatory for >S$50M public tenders since 31 Jan 2025).
details: Lifecycle thinking, compliance evaluation, operational controls.
Often paired with ISO 9001 + 45001 in tri-cert.
Feeds carbon disclosure substance.
regulations.elitez_implication: Strengthens PQM Quality score; relatively low marginal cost once ISO 9001 is in place.

regulations.title: ISO/IEC 17025 — Testing & Calibration Laboratories
regulations.applies_to: Labs performing testing/calibration; relevant to firms that maintain in-house calibration of measurement equipment
regulations.summary: Standard for lab competence. Site-supervision firms typically rely on accredited external labs for instrument calibration to satisfy BCA's calibrated-equipment requirement.
details: Used by SAC-SINGLAS scheme.
Calibration certificates accepted for measurement gear used on site.
Pillar 3 (operational) of the BCA firm regime references calibrated equipment.
regulations.elitez_implication: Procurement signal — ensure all theodolites/total-stations/strain-gauges are calibrated by SAC-SINGLAS labs.

regulations.title: JAC C&S RE / RTO Accreditation
regulations.applies_to: Resident Engineers and Resident Technical Officers supervising civil & structural works
regulations.summary: Since 15 Feb 2009 (Building Control Act amendment) every site supervisor for C&S works must be accredited with the IES-ACES Joint Accreditation Committee. Annual renewal, calendar-year validity, renewable up to 3 years post-expiry.
details: Application processed 2 weeks normal / 6–8 weeks peak.
Renewal fee S$21.80 (incl. 9% GST).
STU (Supervision Training Units) required for renewal.
BCSS (Building Construction Supervisor Safety Course) prerequisite for new RTOs.
Performance Assessment Reports (PAR) issued.
regulations.elitez_implication: Every Elitez-deployed C&S supervisor must hold a current JAC certificate; the firm tracks expiry, STU credits and PAR scores per head.

regulations.title: JAC M&E RE / RTO Accreditation
regulations.applies_to: Resident Engineers and Resident Technical Officers supervising mechanical & electrical works
regulations.summary: Parallel JAC accreditation track for M&E supervision; same annual renewal cadence; STU equivalents in M&E discipline.
details: Same fee structure as C&S track.
Different technical CPD topics (HVAC, electrical, fire-protection).
Required wherever projects involve M&E QP supervision.
regulations.elitez_implication: Multi-discipline firms must staff both C&S and M&E accredited supervisors to bid full-service supervision retainers.

regulations.title: WSQ Framework / Approved Training Organisation (ATO) Status
regulations.applies_to: Organisations delivering nationally recognised WSQ courses
regulations.summary: Workforce Skills Qualifications framework — competency-based national qualifications. Only ATOs accredited by SSG can deliver WSQ courses including Built Environment competency tracks (e.g. BCSS / SCW).
details: ATO assessment covers governance, trainer qualification, course design.
Built Environment WSQ track includes BCSS, Construction Safety Officer.
Funding via SkillsFuture Credit and SCTP.
regulations.elitez_implication: If Elitez chooses to operate its own academy, ATO status becomes the prerequisite; otherwise it partners with an existing ATO (e.g. BCAA, SCAL Academy).

regulations.title: SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme (SCTP)
regulations.applies_to: Singaporeans / PRs ≥ 21 years old transitioning to a new sector or job role
regulations.summary: Train-and-place programme delivered by CET Centres; up to 90% course-fee subsidy + employment-facilitation services. Built Environment SCTP series targets construction reskilling pathways.
details: Part-time or full-time; 3–12 months typical duration.
Delivered by CET Centres (NTU-PaCE, NUS-SCALE, SP, SIT, SMU Academy, BCAA).
Pairs with employment-facilitation services.
regulations.elitez_implication: Direct talent pipeline — Elitez can co-design BE SCTP cohorts with CET Centres to feed RE/RTO trainees on subsidised terms.

regulations.title: Career Conversion Programme (CCP)
regulations.applies_to: Mid-career employers and converts placed into reskilled roles
regulations.summary: Place-and-train programme — employers receive salary support (up to 90% in some tracks) for hiring and reskilling mid-career professionals into in-demand roles, including Built Environment supervision.
details: Typical salary support: 70% (up to 90% for mature converts).
Programme duration 3–12 months.
Built Environment CCPs aligned with SkillsFuture Skills Frameworks.
regulations.elitez_implication: Lets Elitez recoup 70–90% of training-month wages for a mid-career convert (e.g. ex-shipyard supervisor → site RTO).

regulations.title: IHRP Certification (CA / CP / SP / MP)
regulations.applies_to: HR practitioners in Singapore
regulations.summary: Tripartite-set HR credential — IHRP Certified Associate / Certified Professional / Senior Professional / Master Professional. Voluntary but recognised in WSG schemes; SkillsFuture Credit-eligible.
details: Set up by MOM, NTUC, SNEF.
Renewable annually with CPD.
Increasingly cited in HR-services tenders.
regulations.elitez_implication: Elitez Group's HR backbone team should hold IHRP-CP/SP, signalling sophistication to enterprise clients.

regulations.title: Professional Engineer Registration (PEB) — incl. QP(D) and QP(S) Roles
regulations.applies_to: Civil, Structural, Electrical, Mechanical, Chemical engineers practising in Singapore
regulations.summary: PEB registers PEs and issues annual practising certificates required to engage in professional engineering work — including the QP(D) (design) and QP(S) (supervision) roles under the Building Control Act.
details: Approved degree + 4 years relevant practical experience.
Fundamentals of Engineering Examination + Practice of Professional Engineering Examination + Interview.
Annual practising certificate renewal.
40 PDU CPD over the renewal qualifying period.
regulations.elitez_implication: The Technical Director of an Elitez Site Supervision firm must be a registered PE (Civil); annual PC renewal is a critical-path item to monitor.

regulations.title: CSA Cyber Essentials Mark
regulations.applies_to: SMEs and organisations starting their cybersecurity journey
regulations.summary: Entry-tier cyber-baseline mark — recognises basic cyber-hygiene controls. 2-year certification validity. Increasingly required by enterprise/government RFPs.
details: Based on common-attack hygiene controls.
Certification valid 2 years.
Companion to (independent of) Cyber Trust Mark.
regulations.elitez_implication: Reasonable entry-tier target for Elitez Site Supervisor's first 12 months — signals readiness while ISO 27001 is being scoped.

regulations.title: CSA Cyber Trust Mark (SS 712)
regulations.applies_to: Organisations with extensive digitalised operations and higher cyber-risk profiles
regulations.summary: Higher-tier risk-based certification — published as SS 712 in 2025. 3-year cert with annual audits. Cyber Trust (2025) edition extends to Cloud, OT and AI security domains.
details: Risk-based — protection level matches digital exposure.
Up to S$3,600 funding support.
Insurance discount benefits.
regulations.elitez_implication: Aspirational tier — appropriate once Elitez scales digital site-streaming and cloud-recordkeeping infrastructure for the firm regime.

regulations.title: Personal Data Protection Act 2012 — incl. Mandatory Breach Notification (s.26D)
regulations.applies_to: All organisations collecting/using/disclosing personal data in Singapore
regulations.summary: Primary data-protection law. Mandatory data breach notification regime live since 1 Feb 2021 (Section 26D): organisations must notify PDPC within 3 calendar days from determining a breach is notifiable, and notify affected individuals as soon as practicable.
details: Notifiable breach: significant harm to individuals OR ≥ 500 individuals affected.
Maximum financial penalty up to 10% annual turnover (Singapore-derived) or S$1M, whichever higher (effective 1 Oct 2022).
DPO appointment mandatory.
regulations.elitez_implication: Elitez handles supervisor PII, project-site CCTV/biometric and client data — full PDPA compliance + DPO appointment required.

regulations.title: Data Protection Trustmark (DPTM) / SS 714:2025
regulations.applies_to: Voluntary enterprise-wide data-protection certification
regulations.summary: Voluntary cert demonstrating accountable PDPA practice. Formalised as SS 714:2025 under Singapore Standardisation Programme. 4 principles: Governance & Transparency, Management of Personal Data, Care of Personal Data, Individuals' Rights.
details: Issued by IMDA-appointed certification bodies.
Re-certified every 3 years.
Strengthens trust signal in B2B contracts.
regulations.elitez_implication: Optional but worth pursuing 12-18 months after launch — differentiates against competitor supervision firms in PSPC quality scoring.

regulations.title: Green Mark Professional Qualification Scheme (GMA / GMAP / GMAAP)
regulations.applies_to: Built environment professionals supporting Green Mark certified projects
regulations.summary: SGBC-administered scheme replacing the BCA Green Mark Specialist programme. Tiered certification with annual CPD-based renewal.
details: GMA — foundation tier (new entrants/non-technical).
GMAP — central accreditation, 2–3 yrs Green Mark project experience.
GMAAP — advanced tier, ≥ 5 yrs experience.
Annual CPD renewal.
regulations.elitez_implication: GMA-certified RTOs / GMAP-certified REs let Elitez bid Green-Mark-targeted supervision packages credibly.

official_urls.label: BCA — Green Mark Professional Qualification Scheme

regulations.title: WSG-Certified Career Facilitator / Career Coach
regulations.applies_to: Career-services professionals supporting placement & coaching activities
regulations.summary: WSG-recognised credential for individuals delivering career-coaching/placement services in CCP/SCTP and other workforce schemes.
details: Linked to NICF / WSQ Career Services frameworks.
Required for delivery roles in SCTP-funded programmes.
Renewable with CPD.
regulations.elitez_implication: Internal Elitez talent-development team should hold this credential to deliver subsidised SCTP/CCP cohorts.

regulations.title: Singapore Quality Class for People Developer (SQC-PD)
regulations.applies_to: Organisations demonstrating excellence in business and people-development practices
regulations.summary: Recognition framework administered through Enterprise Singapore (with WSG/SSG for the People Developer dimension) — strengthens reputation for organisational excellence.
details: Renewable every 4 years with surveillance.
Often cited in PSPC quality criteria.
Companion frameworks: SQC-Innovation, SQC-Service, SQC-Privacy.
regulations.elitez_implication: Optional reputational badge — useful PSPC quality-score asset when Elitez approaches enterprise clients in 2027/28.

regulations.title: Singapore International Arbitration Centre (SIAC) — Standard Dispute Forum
regulations.applies_to: Construction contracts referring disputes to SIAC arbitration (very common in Singapore practice)
regulations.summary: Most Singapore construction contracts (PSSCOC, REDAS, FIDIC adapted) refer disputes to SIAC. Note: site supervision firms may be drawn into arbitration as joined parties or witnesses.
details: SIAC Rules 2025 in force.
Construction-specific tribunals available.
Adjudication via the Building & Construction Industry Security of Payment Act (SOPA) is the parallel fast-track route.
regulations.elitez_implication: Standard contract clause stack — Elitez's master service agreements should reference SIAC and ensure indemnity carve-outs match supervision-only scope.

regulations.title: Champion of Good (NVPC) — Corporate-Purpose Recognition
regulations.applies_to: Organisations demonstrating sustained corporate-purpose practice
regulations.summary: NVPC-administered recognition for companies with strong corporate-purpose stories. Non-regulatory but stacks into ESG narratives that PQM/PSPC tenders reward.
details: Annual nomination cycle.
Companies share stories on partnerships, programmes, advocacy.
Increasingly visible in supplier-due-diligence questionnaires.
regulations.elitez_implication: Optional — fits Elitez Group's broader social-mobility narrative; standalone supervision firm can lean on the Group's Champion-of-Good positioning.

timeline.event: Building Control Act amended — JAC accreditation mandatory for all C&S and M&E site supervisors (REs/RTOs).
Building Control (Buildability and Productivity) Regulations 2011 commence — minimum B-Score and C-Score for projects ≥ 5,000 m² GFA.
Personal Data Protection Act 2012 main data protection provisions take effect (DPO requirement, consent, access/correction).
SGBC takes over the Green Mark Professional Qualification Scheme (GMA / GMAP / GMAAP) from BCA's GM Specialist programme.
BCA mandates Site Supervision Plan submission for structural and geotechnical works above S$7.5M.
PDPA Section 26D mandatory data breach notification regime takes effect — PDPC must be notified within 3 calendar days.
CSA launches Cyber Essentials Mark and Cyber Trust Mark certifications.
MOM construction sector Dependency Ratio Ceiling reduced from 87.5% (1:7) to 83.3% (1:5); Man-Year Entitlement framework abolished.
SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme (SCTP) re-launched as a national mid-career reskilling pathway across 7 CET Centres.
Mandatory Environmental Sustainability criterion takes effect in BCA's Price Quality Method for public construction tenders > S$50M.
SAC launches Inspection-Body accreditation scheme for Lift & Escalator Certification Firms.
S Pass levy harmonised at flat S$650/month across all sectors and tiers.
BCA announces firm-based licensing regime — L&E certification firms (mandatory 2027) and Site Supervision firms for projects > S$75M (mandatory 2028).
Budget 2026 — PM Wong announces EP/S Pass salary hikes from 1 Jan 2027, LQS to S$1,800 from 1 Jul 2026, PWCS extended to 2028 with 30% co-funding.
Local Qualifying Salary rises from S$1,600 to S$1,800/month — affects DRC headcount calc.
Targeted opening of SAC accreditation scheme for Site Supervision Firms (ISO/IEC 17020 inspection-body track) — voluntary phase begins.
EP minimum salary rises to S$6,000 (S$6,600 finance); S Pass rises to S$3,600 (S$4,000 finance) — applies to new applications. CPF senior-worker rates step up.
BCA Lift & Escalator Certification Firm Licensing becomes mandatory — modern lifts/escalators per SS550/SS626 must engage BCA-licensed firms.
BCA Site Supervision Firm Licensing becomes mandatory for building projects above S$75M — Elitez Site Supervisor target launch quarter.
MOM Work Permit levy framework simplification phase — Manufacturing & Services tier restructuring; salary thresholds extended to renewals.
PWCS scheduled sunset (last qualifying year 2028 at 20% co-funding).
Forecast horizon — by 2030 BCA expects firm-based regimes (SS + L&E) to cover the bulk of large-project supervision, with sector-wide consolidation toward fewer accredited firms.
